context: Tests that the agent detects hardcoded DEPLOY_TOKEN and DB_PASSWORD in the variables block, the curl-pipe-to-bash pattern in the test job, the SSL bypass (curl -k) in the deploy job, and produces a corrected pipeline and structured security report.

checklist.name: Hardcoded DEPLOY_TOKEN detected
checklist.description: security-report.md (or equivalent) flags the DEPLOY_TOKEN variable value as a hardcoded credential

checklist.name: Hardcoded DB_PASSWORD detected
checklist.description: security-report.md flags the DB_PASSWORD variable value as a hardcoded credential

checklist.name: curl-pipe-to-bash detected
checklist.description: security-report.md flags `curl ... | bash` in the test job as an insecure script pattern

checklist.name: SSL bypass detected
checklist.description: security-report.md flags `curl -k` in the deploy job as a TLS certificate verification bypass

checklist.name: Credentials replaced with CI/CD variable references
checklist.description: In the corrected .gitlab-ci.yml, DEPLOY_TOKEN and DB_PASSWORD no longer contain literal values — they reference CI/CD masked/protected variables (e.g., $DEPLOY_TOKEN remains but its value is removed from the variables block, or a comment explains it must be set in GitLab CI/CD settings)

checklist.name: curl-pipe-to-bash remediated
checklist.description: In the corrected .gitlab-ci.yml, the `curl ... | bash` command is replaced or removed (e.g., script downloaded then verified before execution, or replaced with a package install)

checklist.name: SSL bypass remediated
checklist.description: In the corrected .gitlab-ci.yml, the -k flag is removed from the curl command in the deploy job

checklist.name: Severity levels assigned
checklist.description: security-report.md assigns a severity level (CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, or INFO) to each finding

checklist.name: Risk explanation provided
checklist.description: security-report.md explains the risk for at least two findings (e.g., credential exposure in logs, arbitrary code execution, MITM)
